front: According to B V Ramana, what are the four axioms that define a group (G, ∗)?
back: 1. Closure: For all a, b in G, a ∗ b is in G. 2. Associativity: For all a, b, c in G, (a ∗ b) ∗ c = a ∗ (b ∗ c). 3. Identity: There exists an element e in G such that for every a in G, e ∗ a = a ∗ e = a. 4. Inverse: For every a in G, there exists an element a⁻¹ in G such that a ∗ a⁻¹ = a⁻¹ ∗ a = e.

front: What is an Abelian (or commutative) group?
back: A group (G, ∗) is called Abelian if, in addition to the four group axioms, it also satisfies the commutative property: for all a, b in G, a ∗ b = b ∗ a.

front: Define the order of a group and the order of an element within a group.
back: The order of a group (G, ∗) is the number of elements in G, denoted by |G|. The order of an element a in G is the smallest positive integer n such that aⁿ = e (the identity element). If no such n exists, the element is said to have infinite order.

front: What is a subgroup? Provide the necessary and sufficient condition for a subset H of a group G to be a subgroup.
back: A subset H of a group G is a subgroup if H itself forms a group under the same operation. The necessary and sufficient condition is: H is non-empty and for every a, b in H, the element a ∗ b⁻¹ is also in H.
A group G is cyclic if there exists an element g in G (called a generator) such that every element of G can be written as gⁿ for some integer n. That is, G = ⟨g⟩ = { ..., g⁻², g⁻¹, e, g, g², ... }.
